tasks.dependencies: Fetches the Product Master entity list (stores/zones/segments/vendors) to fan out over
One mapped instance per entity, bound to a dedicated Airflow pool
Runs in parallel with process_unit, watches shared pool capacity
Fan-in of all mapped instances plus the monitor
Evaluates whether queue congestion impacted this or sibling DAGs

incident.description: Queue Congestion incident on the Product Master pipeline (RTL_PRD_QUEUE): suppliers vendor-document OCR queue congestion.
incident.symptoms: OCR processing tasks mapped per scanned vendor invoice (up to 1,100/day) queued behind a GPU-limited worker pool for 3+ hours during month-end.
incident.root_cause: The GPU-backed OCR worker pool has only 4 slots, sized for typical daily volume of ~300 invoices, but month-end invoice volume spikes to 1,100+ without any elastic scaling of the pool.
incident.business_impact: Vendor invoice processing backlog delays accounts-payable 3-way match, risking missed early-payment-discount windows worth an estimated $40K/month if consistently missed.
incident.resolution: Add autoscaling to the GPU worker pool tied to queue depth; batch smaller invoices into a single OCR call where the document format allows it to reduce per-call overhead.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: The GPU-backed OCR worker pool has only 4 slots, sized for typical daily volume of ~300 invoices, but month-end invoice volume spikes to 1,100+ without any elastic scaling of the pool.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow shows OCR task instances queued state duration far exceeding their eventual execution duration
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog gpu_pool.queue_depth climbed steadily through month-end days, correlated with the invoice volume metric
expected_rca.resolution: Add autoscaling to the GPU worker pool tied to queue depth; batch smaller invoices into a single OCR call where the document format allows it to reduce per-call overhead.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Add a 'volume seasonality profile' field to the metadata model so month-end-heavy DAGs get proactive pool scaling ahead of known high-volume periods.
